name: Peanut-Butter-Chocolate Shortbread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat margarine or butter until softened.
2. Beat in 1 1/2 cups powdered sugar and vanilla.
3. Add flour, cocoa powder, cornstarch, and salt; beat until smooth.
4. Stir in chopped peanuts. Reserve 1 1/3 cups of the cocoa mixture.
5. Spread the remaining cocoa mixture in the bottom and up the sides of an ungreased 10-inch tart pan with a removable bottom or in the bottom and 1-inch up the sides of a 10-inch springform pan.
6. In a bowl, stir together peanut butter and 1 cup powdered sugar; stir in chocolate pieces.
7. Carefully spread the peanut butter mixture over the crust.
8. Spoon the remaining cocoa mixture over the peanut butter mixture; carefully spread to cover.
9. Using the tines of a fork, score shortbread into 16 wedges.
10. Bake at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for about 50 minutes or until the surface looks slightly dry.
11. Cool slightly. While warm, place a milk chocolate kiss on each wedge.
12. When the kisses soften (about 10 minutes), top with a peanut.
13. Score wedges again.
14. Allow to cool completely before removing sides of pan and cutting into wedges.
